Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (COVID-19 response)
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (COVID-19 response)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (COVID-19 response)

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Procedure and House Affairs as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on JuneÂ 21, 2021

Identification.BillSponsor: PRESIDENT OF THE QUEENâS PRIVY COUNCIL FOR CANADA

Provision.Text: This enactment adds a new Part to the Canada Elections Act that provides for temporary rules to ensure the safe administration of an election in the context of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic. The new Part, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: extends the Chief Electoral Officerâs power to adapt the provisions of that Act to ensure the health or safety of electors or election officers;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorizes a returning officer to constitute polling divisions that consist of a single institution where seniors or persons with a disability reside, or a part of such an institution, and to set the days and hours that a polling station established there will be open;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provides for a polling period of three consecutive days consisting of a Saturday, Sunday and Monday;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provides for the hours of voting during the polling period;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: provides for the opening and closing measures at polling stations;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: sets the days for voting at advance polling stations;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: authorizes the Chief Electoral Officer to modify the day on which certain things are authorized or required to be done before the polling period by moving that day backward or forward by up to two days or the starting date or ending date of a period in which certain things are authorized or required to be done by up to two days;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: provides that an elector may submit an application for registration and special ballot under Division 4 of Part 11 in writing or in electronic form;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: provides that an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in their electoral district may deposit the outer envelope containing their special ballot in a secure reception box or ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes; and

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: prohibits installing a secure reception box for the deposit of outer envelopes unless by or under the authority of the Chief Electoral Officer or a returning officer and prohibits destroying, taking, opening or otherwise interfering with a secure reception box installed by a returning officer.

The enactment also provides for the repeal of the new Part six months after the publication of a notice confirming that the temporary rules in that Part are no longer required to ensure the safe administration of an election in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Canada Elections Act is amended by adding the following after section 555:
Parts 1 to 21 apply, subject to this Part, to an election that is held after the day on which this section comes into force.
This Part applies to an election referred to in section 556.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 17(1) is to be read as follows:

During an election period or within 30 days after it, if an emergency, an unusual or unforeseen circumstance or an error makes it necessary, the Chief Electoral Officer may, for the sole purpose of enabling electors to exercise their right to vote, enabling the counting of votes or ensuring the health or safety of electors or election officers, adapt any provision of this Act and, in particular, may extend the time for doing any act, subject to subsection (2), or may increase the number of election officers or polling stations.
Section 17 is to be read as including the following after subsection (2):

The Chief Electoral Officer shall not prohibit the presence of candidates at polling stations or advance polling stations or, in their absence, the presence of a representative acting on a candidateâs behalf.
Section 17 is to be read as including the following after subsection (2.1):

The Chief Electoral Officer shall not authorize alternative voting processes or voting technology that allows electors to vote, or ballots to be counted, by telephone, Internet or other electronic means.

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to section 66 â subsections (5) to (7)
Section.Text: Section 66 is to be read as including the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: Subject to the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, a requirement under paragraphs (1)(e) to (g) for a signature is satisfied by an electronic signature.
The Chief Electoral Officer may make instructions

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting, for the purposes of subsection (5), the use of electronic signatures; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adapting paragraph (1)(e) or (f) with respect to the requirement for a witness to a signature referred to in those paragraphs if an electronic signature is used, based on the security features of the electronic signature, including by waiving that requirement.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall publish the instructions made under subsection (6) on the Chief Electoral Officerâs Internet site within two days after the day on which the writs are issued.

Heading.TitleText: Signatures of Electors Resident in the Electoral District on Nomination Papers

Section.Text: Paragraphs 66(1)(e) and (f) are to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for any electoral district except one listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures, made in the presence of a witness, of at least 50 electors resident in the electoral district;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for an electoral district listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures, made in the presence of a witness, of at least 25 electors resident in the electoral district;

Heading.TitleText: Voting in Institutions Where Seniors and Persons with a Disability Reside

Section.Text: Paragraph 127(a) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in person at a polling station on polling day or at a polling station established under subsection 561(1);

Section.Text: Subsection 538(5) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: A returning officer may, with the approval of the Chief Electoral Officer, constitute polling divisions that consist of one or more institutions, or a part of an institution, where seniors or persons with a disability reside.

If a polling division consisting of one institution or a part of an institution is constituted under subsection 538(5), the returning officer shall establish a polling station in the institution or part of the institution.

The returning officer shall open a polling station established under subsection (1) for the days and hours during the period that begins on the 13th day before polling day and ends on polling day that the returning officer considers necessary to give electors residing in the institution or part of the institution, as the case may be, a reasonable opportunity to vote, but the polling station is not to be open for a total of more than 12 hours during that period.

The returning officer shall give notice to the candidates of the days and hours that a polling station established under subsection (1) is open in accordance with the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subject to the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, the provisions of this Act that relate to ordinary polls shall, in so far as they are applicable, apply to a polling station established under subsection (1).

Section.MarginalNote: Opening of polling station in institution

Subsection.Text: When the polling station established under subsection 561(1) opens, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the first day that the polling station is open,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: open the ballot box and ascertain that it is empty,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: seal the ballot box with the seals provided by the Chief Electoral Officer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and, subject to section 157, ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on each subsequent day that the polling station is open, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and, subject to section 157, ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Close of polling station in institution
Subsection.Text: At the close of the polling station on each day that it is open, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.
The candidates or their representatives may take note of the serial numbers of the seals on any ballot box used at the polling station at the following times:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: when the ballot box is placed on the table in accordance with subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: when the polling station closes on each day that it is open; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: when the votes are counted on polling day.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 175(5) to (9) apply to a ballot box used at the polling station.

Section.Text: Section 559 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Paragraph 127(a) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in person at a polling station during the polling period or at a polling station established under subsection 561(1);

Section.Text: Subsection 561(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall open a polling station established under subsection (1) for the days and hours during the period that begins on the 13th day before the first day of the polling period and ends on the last day of the polling period that the returning officer considers necessary to give electors residing in the institution or part of the institution, as the case may be, a reasonable opportunity to vote, but the polling station is not to be open for a total of more than 28 hours during that period.

Section.Text: Section 562 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application â definition of polling day

Subsection.Text: The definition polling day in subsection 2(1) does not apply.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Adaptation to definition of pre-election period
Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition pre-election period in subsection 2(1) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the 37th day before the Saturday referred to in subsection 56.1(2). (pÃ©riode prÃ©Ã©lectorale)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) is to be read as including the following definition in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: polling period, in relation to an election, means the period fixed for voting at the election under paragraph 57(1.2)(c) or subsection 59(4) or 77(2).â(pÃ©riode du scrutin)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(6) is to be read as follows:

If a writ for an election is withdrawn under subsection 59(1) or is deemed to be withdrawn under subsection 31(3) of the Parliament of Canada Act, then, for the purposes of Parts 16.1 and 17 and Divisions 1, 2, 4 and 5 of Part 18, the last day of the polling period is deemed to be the day that the writ is withdrawn or deemed to be withdrawn.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(3) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: If voting at a polling station is interrupted on any day of the polling period by an emergency and the Chief Electoral Officer is satisfied that, if the voting hours at the polling station are not extended, a substantial number of electors will not be able to vote, the Chief Electoral Officer shall extend the voting hours at the polling station for the period the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to give those electors a reasonable opportunity to vote, as long as the polling station does not in any case

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: close later than midnight on any day of the polling period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: remain open during the polling period for a total of more than 28 hours.

Section.Text: Subsection 56.1(2) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1), each general election must be held on the Saturday, Sunday and Monday that follow the second Monday of October in the fourth calendar year following the last general election.

Section.Text: Section 56.2 does not apply.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 57(1.2)(c) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fix the polling period, the first day of which must be no earlier than the 36th day and no later than the 50th day after the day on which the writ was issued.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 57(2)(b) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the polling period shall be the same for all electoral districts; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 57(3) is to be read as follows:
The polling period shall be three consecutive days consisting of a Saturday, Sunday and Monday.
Subsections 57(4) and (5) do not apply.
Subsection 59(3) is to be read as follows:

The period named in the new writ for the polling period is determined by the Governor in Council, but the first day of the period may be no later than the 50th day after the day on which the new writ was issued.
In subsection 59(4), the expression âdate for the new polling dayâ is to be read as âdates for the new polling periodâ.
Paragraph 59(5)(a) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done on a day that is a certain number of days before the first day of the polling period or done within a period that ends before the first day of the polling period, and the day occurs or the period ends before the day on which the postponement order is made, then the first day of the polling period is deemed to still be the first day of the period fixed under paragraph 57(1.2)(c) and not the first day of the period fixed under subsection (4);

Subsection.MarginalNote: Adaptation to portion of paragraph 59(5)(b) before subparagraph (i)
Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 59(5)(b) before subparagraph (i) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done within a period that ends on the last day of the polling period or before that day, and the period in which the thing is authorized or required to be done ends on or after the day on which the postponement order is made, then

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 59(5)(e) does not apply.

Section.Text: Paragraph 62(b) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the dates for the polling period;

Section.Text: Section 77 is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Postponement of closing day for nominations on death of candidate

Subsection.Text: If a candidate endorsed by a registered party dies after 2:00 p.m. on the 5th day before the closing day for nominations and before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period, the election is postponed and the returning officer shall, after communicating with the Chief Electoral Officer, fix the 2nd Saturday after the death as the closing day for nominations in that electoral district.

Notice of the day fixed under subsection (1) shall be given by a further Notice of Election distributed and posted as specified by the Chief Electoral Officer, and there shall be named by the Notice of Election a new polling period, the first day of which shall be Saturday, the 21st day after the day fixed under that subsection.
The lists of electors to be used at a postponed election shall be the lists of electors that were revised before the 6th day before the first day of the new polling period.

Section.Text: Section 120 is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: Each returning officer shall, for the polling period, establish polling stations and assign each polling division to a polling station.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Same premises for entire polling period
Subsection.Text: A polling station shall be in the same premises on each day of the polling period unless the returning officer receives the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer to establish the polling station in different premises for one or more days during that period.

Section.Text: The heading âPolling Dayâ after section 127 is to be read as âPolling Periodâ.
Section 128 is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Hours of voting â first and second day of polling period

Subsection.Text: The voting hours on the first and second day of the polling period are from 9:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Hours of voting â last day of polling period
Subsection.Text: The voting hours on the last day of the polling period are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from 8:30 a.m. to 8:30 p.m., if the electoral district is in the Newfoundland, Atlantic or Central time zone;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: from 9:30 a.m. to 9:30 p.m., if the electoral district is in the Eastern time zone;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: from 7:30 a.m. to 7:30 p.m., if the electoral district is in the Mountain time zone; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: from 7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m., if the electoral district is in the Pacific time zone.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (2), if any day of the polling period falls during a time of year when daylight saving time is observed in the majority of electoral districts, daylight saving time is deemed to be observed on that day in any electoral district in which daylight saving time is not observed during that time of year.

For the purpose of subsection (2), if any day of the polling period falls during a time of year when standard time is observed in the majority of electoral districts, standard time is deemed to be observed on that day in any electoral district in which standard time is not observed during that time of year.

Section.Text: Section 131 is to be read as follows:
If only one by-election is held or if more than one by-election is held during the same polling period and all of them are in the same time zone, the hours of voting are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the first and second day of the polling period, from 9:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the last day of the polling period, from 8:30 a.m. to 8:30 p.m.

Section.Text: Section 140 is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: When the polling station opens, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the first day that the polling station is open,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: open the ballot box and ascertain that it is empty,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: seal the ballot box with the seals provided by the Chief Electoral Officer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on each subsequent day that the polling station is open, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day.

Subsection.Text: At the close of the polling station on each day that it is open, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.
The candidates or their representatives may take note of the serial numbers of the seals on any ballot box used at the polling station at the following times:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: when the ballot box is placed on the table in accordance with subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: when the polling station closes on each day that it is open; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: when the votes are counted on the last day of the polling period.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 175(5) to (9) apply to a ballot box used at the polling station.

Section.Text: The heading before section 161 is to be read as âPolling Period Registrationâ.
Section 162 is to be read as including the following after paragraph (i.1):

Paragraph.Label: (i.11)
Paragraph.Text: provide, on request, the documents referred to in paragraph (i.1) that were prepared on the first day and the second day of the polling period to a candidateâs representative at the close of the polling station on the day those documents were prepared;

Section.Text: Subsection 171(2) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When advance polling stations to be open
Subsection.Text: An advance polling station shall be open from 9:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. on Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday, the 9th, 8th, 7th and 6th days, respectively, before the first day of the polling period, and shall not be open at any other time.

Section.Text: In subsection 176(1), the expression âMonday the 7th dayâ is to be read as âSunday, the 6th dayâ.
In subsection 204(2), the expression âthe date of polling dayâ is to be read as âthe dates of the polling periodâ.

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to subsection 214(1), sections 229, 239 and 261, etc.
Section.Text: The following paragraphs apply if the last day of the polling period is a holiday:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 214(1) is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Information to be provided to elector

Subsection.Text: The unit election officer shall inform an elector that, in order for the special ballot to be counted, the outer envelope must be sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period. The unit election officer shall inform the elector of the service provided by the Canadian Forces to deliver the outer envelope.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 229 is to be read as follows:

Section.Text: The special ballot must be sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period in order to be counted.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 239(1) before paragraph (a) is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Sending and receiving special ballot â application made outside electoral district

Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the special voting rules administrator or by a returning officer outside the electorâs electoral district shall ensure that the special ballot is sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and is received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period. The special ballot shall be transmitted by

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 239(2) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Receipt of special ballot â application made in electoral district
Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district shall ensure that the ballot is sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and is received at the office of that returning officer no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period. The special ballot shall be transmitted by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sending the sealed outer envelope to the returning officer by mail or any other means; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: depositing the sealed outer envelope

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a secure reception box installed under paragraph (2.1)(a) or subparagraph (2.1)(b)(i), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes installed under subparagraph (2.1)(b)(ii).

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 239(3) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), a special ballot referred to in that subsection that is sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period shall be counted.

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 261 is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Deadline for return of election material
Section.Text: Every liaison officer shall ensure that the election material referred to in section 260 is sent before the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period and received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period.

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 267(1) is to be read as including the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: the outer envelope was sent after the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 267(1)(d) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the outer envelope has been received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region after 6:00 p.m. on the Tuesday following the last day of the polling period; or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 277(1) is to be read as including the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: the outer envelope was sent after the close of polling stations on the last day of the polling period; or

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to section 477.761 of French version
Section.Text: In section 477.761 of the French version of this Act, the expression âÃ la dateâ is to be read as âau dernier jourâ.

Section.MarginalNote: Modification â day thing authorized or required to be done

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may, within two days after the issue of the writ for an election,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done on a day that is a certain number of days after the issue of the writ or before the first day of the polling period, modify that day by moving it forward or backward, unless that day is the closing day for nominations, by up to two days; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done within a period that ends before the first day of the polling period, modify the starting date or the ending date of that period, unless that starting date or ending date is the closing day for nominations, by up to two days.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall cause a notice of any modification to be published without delay in the Canada Gazette and on the Chief Electoral Officerâs Internet site.

Section.MarginalNote: Before the first day of the polling period

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and 567(4), the expression âbefore polling dayâ in Parts 1 to 21 is to be read as âbefore the first day of the polling periodâ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â before the last day of the polling period
Subsection.Text: The expression âbefore polling dayâ is to be read as âbefore the last day of the polling periodâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 335(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 345(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 348(a).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â paragraph 359(4)(a) of French version
Subsection.Text: In paragraph 359(4)(a) of the French version of this Act, the expression âprÃ©cÃ©dant le jour du scrutinâ is to be read as âprÃ©cÃ©dant le dernier jour de la pÃ©riode du scrutinâ.

Section.MarginalNote: After the last day of the polling period
Section.Text: Subject to section 588, the expressions âafter polling dayâ and âafter the polling dayâ in Parts 1 to 21 are to be read as âafter the last day of the polling periodâ.

Section.MarginalNote: The last day of the polling period

Subsection.Text: Subject to the other provisions of this Part, the expression âpolling dayâ in Parts 1 to 21 is to be read as âthe last day of the polling periodâ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â during the polling period
Subsection.Text: The expression âon polling dayâ is to be read as âduring the polling periodâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 17(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 106;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 132(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 135(1) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 158(3);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 161(1) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 161(5.1);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 162(i.1);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: section 165;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 171(1);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 174(2);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 533(a.1); and

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: section 541.1.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â subsection 133(2) of English version
Subsection.Text: In subsection 133(2) of the English version of this Act, the expression âon polling dayâ is to be read as âduring the polling periodâ.
In paragraph 489(3)(b.1), the expression âpolling dayâ is to be read as âpolling periodâ.
In paragraph 119(1)(g), the expression âpolling dayâ is to be read as âthe polling periodâ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exception â each day of the polling period
Subsection.Text: The expression âpolling dayâ is to be read as âeach day of the polling periodâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 95(2)(b); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 138(1).

Subsection.Text: In subsection 477.49(2), the expression âpolling dayâ is to be read as âan electionâ.

Section.Text: In Parts 1 to 21, the expressions âa day setâ and âthe day setâ are to be read as âthe days setâ.
Any mention of a number of days before the first day of the polling period prevails over any mention of a day of the week corresponding to that number of days.

Subsection.Text: The following paragraphs apply to the first general election for which the election period begins after the day on which this section comes into force, and any by-election for which the election period begins before that general election:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 357.01(1)(b) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has incurred expenses referred to in subsection 349.1(1) or 350(1) in an aggregate amount of $10,000 or more during the period beginning on the day after polling day at the previous general election and ending on the 23rd day before the first day of the polling period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 357.02(1)(b) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has incurred expenses referred to in subsection 349.1(1) or 350(1) in an aggregate amount of $10,000 or more during the period beginning on the day after polling day at the previous general election and ending on the 9th day before the first day of the polling period; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 359(4)(a) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, by class of contributor, of contributions for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes that were received during the period beginning the day after polling day at the general election previous to the last day of the polling period referred to in subsection (1) and ending on that last day of the polling period referred to in that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in paragraph 349.91(5)(b), subparagraph 357.01(5)(a)(ii) and paragraph 359(4.1)(b), a reference to paragraph 57(1.2)(c) is deemed to be a reference to that paragraph as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 584 does not apply to the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 349.91(1)(a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 349.92(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 585 does not apply to the following provisions:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 349.91(5)(b),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph 357.01(5)(a)(ii), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 359(4.1)(b).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the provisions referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) and subparagraphs (1)(e)(i) and (ii) and (f)(i) to (iii), polling day has the same meaning as it had immediately before the day on which this section comes into force.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 588:
Subsection 232(1) is to be read as follows:

Section.MarginalNote: Conditions for voting by special ballot

Subsection.Text: An elector may vote under this Division if his or her application for registration and special ballot, which may be submitted in writing or in electronic form, is received by a returning officer in an electoral district or by the special voting rules administrator after the issue of the writs but before 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before the first day of the polling period.

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to Division 4 of Part 11 â section 232.1
Section.Text: Division 4 of Part 11 is to be read as including the following after section 232:

Subsection.Text: An elector may obtain an application for registration and special ballot at any post office in Canada.

For the purposes of subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the Canada Post Corporation with applications for registration and special ballot and information for the public about voting under this Division.

Section.Text: Paragraph 233(1)(c) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: satisfactory proof of the electorâs identity and residence, which may consist of information that, in the opinion of the Chief Electoral Officer, allows for an electorâs identity or residence to be established because it correlates with information referred to in subsection 46(1.1) that the Chief Electoral Officer holds for that elector;

Section.Text: Section 235 is to be read as follows:
Once an electorâs application for registration and special ballot has been accepted, the elector may vote only under this Division unless the elector

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is authorized by the returning officer to vote other than under this Division;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: returns the special ballot that the elector received to a designated election officer in person at

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the office of the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the electorâs advance polling station, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the electorâs polling station; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 239(2) is to be read as follows:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Receipt of special ballot â application made in electoral district
Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district shall ensure that the ballot is received at the office of that returning officer before the close of the polling stations on the last day of the polling period. The special ballot shall be transmitted by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sending the sealed outer envelope to the returning officer by mail or any other means; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: depositing the sealed outer envelope

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in a secure reception box installed under paragraph (2.1)(a) or subparagraph (2.1)(b)(i), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes installed under subparagraph (2.1)(b)(ii).

Subsection.Text: Section 239 is to be read as including the following after subsection (2):
The returning officer shall install

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a secure reception box outside the returning officerâs office; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following receptacles at each advance polling station and each polling station in the electoral district:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a secure reception box outside the advance polling station or polling station, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes inside the advance polling station or polling station.

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to Division 4 of Part 11 â section 243.2
Section.Text: Division 4 of Part 11 is to be read as including the following after section 243.1:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall inform post-secondary educational institutions that offices for voting by special ballot may be established on their premises.
A returning officer shall establish an office for voting by special ballot on the premises of a post-secondary educational institution in that returning officerâs electoral district if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an office for voting by special ballot was established on its premises for the 2019 general election and the institution agrees to the establishment of the office; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the institution requests the establishment of an office for voting by special ballot on its premises and the returning officer is of the opinion that an office may be established for that purpose.

Subsection.Text: An elector who applies in person to vote at an office established under subsection (2) shall be given a special ballot and may vote by following the procedure for voting by special ballot and return the outer envelope to an election officer at such an office.

An elector who has received a special ballot, other than an elector referred to in subsection (3), may vote in person at an office established under subsection (2) by following the procedure for voting by special ballot and return their outer envelope to an election officer at that office.

An office established under subsection (2) may be open during the period that begins on the 18th day before the first day of the polling period and ends at 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before the first day of the polling period, but the office is not to be open for a total of more than 28 hours during that period.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Office deemed office of returning officer
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, an office established under subsection (2) in an electoral district is deemed to be the office of the returning officer in that electoral district.
For the purposes of this section, the Chief Electoral Officer may issue any instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary in order to execute its purposes.
Subsection 281.7(1) is to be read as including the following after paragraph (h):

Paragraph.Label: (h.1)
Paragraph.Text: install a secure reception box for the deposit of outer envelopes unless by or under the authority of the Chief Electoral Officer or a returning officer; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 281.7(1)(i) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: destroy, take, open or otherwise interfere with a ballot box, secure reception box installed under subsection 239(2.1) or book or packet of ballots or special ballots otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.MarginalNote: Adaptation to portion of subsection 549.1(1) before paragraph (a)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 549.1(1) before paragraph (a) is to be read as follows:

For the purposes of subsections 143(3) and (3.2), sections 144 and 147 and paragraphs 161(1)(b), 169(2)(b) and 235(c), the solemn declaration by which an elector proves his or her identity and residence, proves his or her residence only, proves that he or she is qualified as an elector or proves that he or she has not previously voted at the election shall be in the prescribed form, which shall include the statements that
Paragraph 549.1(1)(d) is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the elector has not previously voted in the election and will not vote more than once in the election, including by using a special ballot.

Section.Text: Part 22 of the Act is repealed.

Section.MarginalNote: Elections after repeal of Part 22 of Canada Elections Act

Subsection.Text: The following paragraphs apply to the first general election for which the election period begins after the day on which section 6 comes into force and any by-election for which the election period begins before that general election if the previous general election was held during a three-day period:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 357.01(1)(b) of the Canada Elections Act is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has incurred expenses referred to in subsection 349.1(1) or 350(1) in an aggregate amount of $10,000 or more during the period beginning on the day after the last day of the polling period at the previous general election and ending on the 23rd day before polling day; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 357.02(1)(b) of the Canada Elections Act is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: has incurred expenses referred to in subsection 349.1(1) or 350(1) in an aggregate amount of $10,000 or more during the period beginning on the day after the last day of the polling period at the previous general election and ending on the 9th day before polling day; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 359(4)(a) of the Canada Elections Act is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, by class of contributor, of contributions for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes that were received during the period beginning the day after the last day of the polling period at the general election previous to the polling day referred to in subsection (1) and ending on that polling day referred to in that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in paragraph 349.91(5)(b), subparagraph 357.01(5)(a)(ii) and paragraph 359(4.1)(b) of the Canada Elections Act, a reference to paragraph 57(1.2)(c) is deemed to be a reference to that paragraph as it read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the expression âpolling dayâ is to be read as âthe last day of the polling periodâ in the following provisions of the Canada Elections Act:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraphs 349.91(1)(a) and (5)(b),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 349.92(1)(a),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph 357.01(5)(a)(ii), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 359(4.1)(b).

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the provisions referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) and (e), polling period has the same meaning as it had immediately before the day on which this section comes into force.
Words and expressions used in this section have the same meaning as in the Canada Elections Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Coming into force during election period â section 2

Subsection.Text: If section 2 of this Act comes into force during an election period, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Elections Act, that Act, as it read immediately before the day on which that section 2 comes into force, applies with respect to that election and all related rights and obligations.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Coming into force during election period â section 6
Subsection.Text: If section 6 of this Act comes into force during an election period, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Elections Act, that Act, as it read immediately before the day on which that section 6 comes into force, applies with respect to that election and all related rights and obligations.

Section.MarginalNote: Election within six months â amendments apply
Section.Text: Despite subsection 554(1) of the Canada Elections Act, the amendments to that Act enacted by sections 1 to 5 of this Act apply to an election for which the writ is issued within six months after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

Sections 2 to 5 come into force on the 90th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent unless, before that day, the Chief Electoral Officer publishes a notice in the Canada Gazette that the necessary preparations for the bringing into operation of those sections have been made and that they may come into force accordingly, in which case those sections come into force on the day on which the notice is published.

Section.MarginalNote: Six months after publication of notice

Subsection.Text: Sections 6 and 7 come into force on the day that, in the sixth month after the month during which the Chief Electoral Officer publishes the notice described in subsection (2) in the Canada Gazette, has the same calendar number as the day on which that notice is published or, if that sixth month has no day with that number, the last day of that sixth month.

The Chief Electoral Officer must consult the Chief Public Health Officer, appointed under subsection 6(1) of the Public Health Agency of Canada Act, before publishing the notice, and the notice must indicate that the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that the temporary rules in Part 22 of the Canada Elections Act are no longer required to ensure the safe administration of an election in the context of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic.

If, after publishing the notice described in subsection (2) but before the day on which sections 6 and 7 come into force under subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer publishes a notice in the Canada Gazette that the necessary preparations for the bringing into operation of those sections have been made and that they may come into force accordingly, those sections come into force on the day on which that notice is published.
